title: Fluorescence Microscopy of Chlamydomonas reinhardtii - mCherry and Chlorophyll
author: João itor Molino
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.kfrctm6

steps.title: Grow cells to late-log phase density...
steps.instruction: Grow cells to late-log phase density (~1 × 10 7 cells/mL) in Tris-acetate-phosphate (TAP) medium at 25°C under constant illumination of 50 μmol photons/m 2 s at 150 rpm on a rotary shaker

steps.title: Prepare a 1% agarose TAP media,...
steps.instruction: Prepare a 1% agarose TAP media, by adding sufficient agarose to TAP media and dissolve agarose by heating in a microwave. Pour the melted media in a agar pad slide, or prepare a glass slide with a double adjacent slides barrier, forming a wall around the pad slide. Add slides on top of the poured media to allow formation of a smooth surface Remove adjacent and top slides, and add 10 µL of culture to a desired spot on the solid media Add a coverslip above the sample a

steps.title: Place slide on microscope Use a...
steps.instruction: Place slide on microscope Use a argon laser at 543 nm to excite mCherry and a spectral detector set at 610-650 nm. Record images.

steps.title: Place slide on microscope Use a...
steps.instruction: Place slide on microscope Use a laser at 405 nm to excite chlorophyll and a spectral detector set at 680 nm. Record images.

steps.title: For stack images use 0
steps.instruction: For stack images use 0.4 µm steps per picture in z-axis
